description: http:// or https:// URLs in JS string literals break comment-stripping brace-balance checkers. The // triggers the //.*$ comment regex, truncating the line and leaving an unclosed string.
example_fix:
const ns = "http:\x2f\x2fwww.w3.org\x2f2000\x2fsvg";
// or: const ns = 'http:' + '//www.w3.org/2000/svg';

description: navigator.clipboard.writeText() fails silently on HTTP (non-HTTPS) contexts or when permissions are denied. An empty .catch() or no fallback means copy silently fails.
Calling getBoundingClientRect() or getComputedStyle() inside render loops or hot paths causes forced synchronous reflow, killing performance at 60fps.
Event listeners attached with addEventListener are never removed, or setInterval/setTimeout IDs are not tracked for cleanup, causing memory leaks and ghost callbacks.
Setting el.className = 'value' destroys ALL existing classes including animation, state, and dynamic classes. Use classList.add/remove instead.
example_fix:
cell.classList.remove('player1', 'player2');
cell.classList.add('player1');

description: Using for...in to iterate object keys is vulnerable to prototype pollution in environments where you cannot control the global scope. Use Object.keys() or Object.entries() instead.
Using || for numeric defaults treats 0 as falsy, making zero an impossible input value. parseInt(v) || 32 silently converts 0 to 32.
example_bad:
const shift = parseInt(input.value) || 3;
const size = parseFloat(slider.value) || 32;

description: WebGL fragment/vertex shaders must be deleted with gl.deleteShader() after linking the program. Otherwise GPU memory leaks on every recompile.
requestAnimationFrame pauses when the tab is hidden but performance.now() keeps counting. On return, the delta can be minutes, causing physics explosions or instant timeouts.
Compiling regex with new RegExp() inside a render loop or hot path wastes CPU. Pre-compile regex outside the loop.
User input or external data injected into innerHTML without escaping HTML entities enables XSS attacks. Even 'safe' data can contain <, >, &, quotes.
Game reset or state change does not clear pending setTimeout/setInterval callbacks, causing ghost callbacks that corrupt the new state.
Handling pointerdown and pointerup but not pointercancel leaves drag state stuck when the OS interrupts a gesture (notification, orientation change).
Web Audio exponentialRampToValueAtTime() throws DOMException when gain is exactly 0. Use setTargetAtTime() for release envelopes, or clamp starting value to >= 0.001.
Creating canvas elements or calling createElement('canvas') inside a render loop leaks memory and will crash the tab by exceeding the browser's canvas context limit.
requestAnimationFrame loops that can be re-triggered without cancelling the previous frame ID cause exponentially multiplying concurrent animation loops.
toISOString().slice(0,10) returns UTC date which can be a different day than the user's local date. Use getFullYear/getMonth/getDate for local dates.
example_fix:
const d = new Date();
const today = d.getFullYear() + '-' + String(d.getMonth()+1).padStart(2,'0') + '-' + String(d.getDate()).padStart(2,'0');

description: String.split('') breaks UTF-16 surrogate pairs (emojis, CJK extension B+). Use [...str] or Array.from(str) for character-level operations.
example_fix:
const chars = [...text];
// or: const chars = Array.from(text);

description: Attaching mouseup/pointerup to canvas only means drags that escape the canvas boundary leave the drag state stuck. Attach to window instead.
example_fix: window.addEventListener('mouseup', onUp);

description: Arrays that grow with push() every frame without a cap will eventually crash. Trail arrays, particle arrays, and history arrays need maximum length enforcement.
DOM queries (getElementById, querySelector, querySelectorAll) inside render loops or hot paths trigger layout recalculation every frame. Cache element references at init.
AudioContext starts in suspended state on iOS/Safari. Failing to call audioCtx.resume() after creation results in permanent silence on mobile browsers.
example_fix:
const ctx = new AudioContext();
ctx.resume();

description: Using 'a' before vowel-starting words ('a orange', 'a elegant') is a grammar error. Apply regex-based a/an correction for user-facing text.
URL.createObjectURL() creates persistent blob URLs. Not calling revokeObjectURL() after use causes memory leaks that will eventually crash the tab.
Web Audio createBuffer() requires integer length. sampleRate * duration can produce non-integer values, crashing with an error.
example_fix: const buf = ctx.createBuffer(1, Math.floor(ctx.sampleRate * 0.15), ctx.sampleRate);

description: Custom markdown parsers must extract code blocks BEFORE running bold/italic/heading transforms. Otherwise inline patterns corrupt code content.
